Package cityrescue

Class CityRescueImpl

java.lang.Object
cityrescue.CityRescueImpl
All Implemented Interfaces:
CityRescue
public class CityRescueImpl extends Object implements CityRescue
CityRescueImpl.java is the main implementation of the CityRescue interface. It is used to handle all the rescue operations, units, stations, incidents, obstacles, and handles the resolutions to incidents over time. This class tracks the units, stations and incidents. It allows removal or addition of stations, increasing and decommissioning units, reporting and cancelling incidents and finally has time ticks.

    Adds a unit to the station.

    Dispatches an available unit to the reported incident.
    void
    escalateIncident(int incidentId, int newSeverity)
    Escalates the severity of the incident which must be between 1 and 5, and the incident must not be resolved or cancelled.
    int[]
    getGridSize()
    Returns the current size of the city as an array [width, height]

    getIncidentIds()
    Returns the IDs of the incidents in ascending order.
    int[]
    getStationIds()
    Returns the IDs of all the stations in ascending order.
    String
    getStatus()
    Returns the status of the city which includes the number of stations, units, incidents, obstacles, and the ticks.
    int[]
    getUnitIds()
    Returns all the IDs of the units in ascending order
    void
    initialise(int width, int height)
    Initialises city rescue with a predefined map size.
    void
    removeObstacle(int x, int y)
    Removes an obstacle in the coordinates selected within the map grid.
    void
    removeStation(int stationId)
    Removes a station's ID as long as it doesn't have any units assigned to it anymore.
    int
    reportIncident(IncidentType type, int severity, int x, int y)
    Reports an incident, including the type, the severity and the location.
    void
    setStationCapacity(int stationId, int maxUnits)
    Sets the capacity of a station.
    void
    setUnitOutOfService(int unitId, boolean outOfService)
    Sets a unit's status as out of service or idle.
    void
    tick()
    Introduces a tick system which keeps track of the time units spend at incident locations and solving them, updates the units status during, after an incident tracking their work progress in the meantime.
    void
    transferUnit(int unitId, int newStationId)
    Transfers a unit from current station to new station.
    String
    viewIncident(int incidentId)
    Returns a string with the incident ID, the incident type, the severity, the location of the incident, the status of the incident and the unit ID to which the current incident is resolved by.
    String
    viewUnit(int unitId)
    Returns a string with the unit ID, the unit type, the home station ID, the location of the unit, the status of the unit, the incident ID to which the current unit is responding and the work tick, which is only available if the unit is at the scene.

  • Constructor Details

    • CityRescueImpl

      public CityRescueImpl()

  • Method Details

    • initialise

      public void initialise(int width, int height) throws InvalidGridException
      Initialises city rescue with a predefined map size. Sets up a grid of the map empty and stores stations, units and incidents in arrays. Begins with 0 incidents, stations and units and sets tick to 0
      Specified by:
      initialise in interface CityRescue
      Parameters:
      width - width of the grid
      height - height of the grid
      Throws:
      InvalidGridException - if grid is invalid or not within the restrictions

    • getGridSize

      public int[] getGridSize()
      Returns the current size of the city as an array [width, height]
      Specified by:
      getGridSize in interface CityRescue
      Returns:
      array with the width and height of the grid

    • addObstacle

      public void addObstacle(int x, int y) throws InvalidLocationException
      Adds an obstacle in the coordinates selected within the map grid. Checks that the chosen coordinates are within the boundaries otherwise, throws an exception.
      Specified by:
      addObstacle in interface CityRescue
      Parameters:
      x - the x coordinate in the map where the obstacle is to be added in
      y - the y coordinate in the map where the obstacle is to be added in
      Throws:
      InvalidLocationException - if coordinates are not within the bounds

    • removeObstacle

      public void removeObstacle(int x, int y) throws InvalidLocationException
      Removes an obstacle in the coordinates selected within the map grid. Checks that the chosen coordinates are within the boundaries, otherwise throws an exception.
      Specified by:
      removeObstacle in interface CityRescue
      Parameters:
      x - the x coordinate in the map where the obstacle is to be removed in
      y - the y coordinate in the map where the obstacle is to be removed in
      Throws:
      InvalidLocationException - if coordinates are not within the bounds

    • addStation

      public int addStation(String name, int x, int y) throws InvalidNameException, InvalidLocationException
      Adds a station in the coordinates selected within the map grid. Checks to see if the name of the station is not blank, the location is within the boundaries, and that maximum number of stations has not been exceeded, otherwise throws an exception.
      Specified by:
      addStation in interface CityRescue
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the new station
      x - the new x coordinate of the station
      y - the new y coordinate of the station
      Returns:
      ID of the new station
      Throws:
      InvalidNameException - if the station name is empty or null
      InvalidLocationException - if the station location is not within the bounds or is blocked

    • removeStation

      public void removeStation(int stationId) throws IDNotRecognisedException, IllegalStateException
      Removes a station's ID as long as it doesn't have any units assigned to it anymore.
      Specified by:
      removeStation in interface CityRescue
      Parameters:
      stationId - the unique identification of the station to be removed
      Throws:
      IDNotRecognisedException - if the station is not found or recognised
      IllegalStateException - if the station still owns units

    • setStationCapacity

      public void setStationCapacity(int stationId, int maxUnits) throws IDNotRecognisedException, InvalidCapacityException
      Sets the capacity of a station. Is invalid if the new capacity is less than the current unit count.
      Specified by:
      setStationCapacity in interface CityRescue
      Parameters:
      stationId - the unique identification of the station
      maxUnits - new maximum unit capacity in the station
      Throws:
      IDNotRecognisedException - if the ID is not recognised
      InvalidCapacityException - if the new capacity is 0 or less than the number of units

    • getStationIds

      public int[] getStationIds()
      Returns the IDs of all the stations in ascending order.
      Specified by:
      getStationIds in interface CityRescue
      Returns:
      an array of the station IDs

    • addUnit

      public int addUnit(int stationId, UnitType type) throws IDNotRecognisedException, InvalidUnitException, IllegalStateException
      Adds a unit to the station. Throws an exception if the unit is null, station ID cannot be found or if maximum capacity has been met.
      Specified by:
      addUnit in interface CityRescue
      Parameters:
      stationId - the unique identification of the station the unit is to be added to
      type - type of the unit to be added in the station
      Returns:
      the ID of the new unit
      Throws:
      IDNotRecognisedException - if the station ID is not recognised
      InvalidUnitException - if the unit type to be added in the station is invalid
      IllegalStateException - if the station has no free capacity

    • decommissionUnit

      public void decommissionUnit(int unitId) throws IDNotRecognisedException, IllegalStateException
      Decommissions a unit based on its ID. Cannot occur if the unit is busy.
      Specified by:
      decommissionUnit in interface CityRescue
      Parameters:
      unitId - the unique identification of the unit to be decommissioned
      Throws:
      IDNotRecognisedException - if the ID is not recognised
      IllegalStateException - if the unit cannot be decommissioned

    • transferUnit

      public void transferUnit(int unitId, int newStationId) throws IDNotRecognisedException, IllegalStateException
      Transfers a unit from current station to new station. The unit must be idle and the station must not be full.
      Specified by:
      transferUnit in interface CityRescue
      Parameters:
      unitId - gets the unique identification of the unit to be transferred
      newStationId - gets the unique identification of the station to transfer the unit to
      Throws:
      IDNotRecognisedException - if the unit's ID or station is not recognised
      IllegalStateException - if the unit is not idle or the station is full

    • setUnitOutOfService

      public void setUnitOutOfService(int unitId, boolean outOfService) throws IDNotRecognisedException, IllegalStateException
      Sets a unit's status as out of service or idle. Units must be idle to go out of service and vice versa.
      Specified by:
      setUnitOutOfService in interface CityRescue
      Parameters:
      unitId - gets the unique identification of the unit
      outOfService - returns a boolean indicating whether unit is go out of service or idle
      Throws:
      IDNotRecognisedException - if unit ID is not recognised
      IllegalStateException - if unit cannot be set out of service or back to idle

    • getUnitIds

      public int[] getUnitIds()
      Returns all the IDs of the units in ascending order
      Specified by:
      getUnitIds in interface CityRescue
      Returns:
      array of the units Ids

    • viewUnit

      public String viewUnit(int unitId) throws IDNotRecognisedException
      Returns a string with the unit ID, the unit type, the home station ID, the location of the unit, the status of the unit, the incident ID to which the current unit is responding and the work tick, which is only available if the unit is at the scene.
      Specified by:
      viewUnit in interface CityRescue
      Parameters:
      unitId - the unique Identification of the unit to view
      Returns:
      string containing information regarding the unit
      Throws:
      IDNotRecognisedException - if the unit ID is not recognised

    • reportIncident

      public int reportIncident(IncidentType type, int severity, int x, int y) throws InvalidSeverityException, InvalidLocationException
      Reports an incident, including the type, the severity and the location. Throws an exception if the incident is null, severity is out of the range, the location is out of the bounds, the location is blocked or if the maximum number of incidents has been reached.
      Specified by:
      reportIncident in interface CityRescue
      Parameters:
      type - the type of incident
      severity - the severity of the incident
      x - the x coordinate of the incident location
      y - the y coordinate of the incident location
      Returns:
      the ID of a new accident being created
      Throws:
      InvalidSeverityException - if the severity is out of range
      InvalidLocationException - if the location is out of the bounds or is blocked

    • cancelIncident

      public void cancelIncident(int incidentId) throws IDNotRecognisedException, IllegalStateException
      Cancels an incident by its ID unless it has already been resolved or in progress. If dispatched, the assigned unit is set to idle.
      Specified by:
      cancelIncident in interface CityRescue
      Parameters:
      incidentId - the unique identification of the incident to cancel
      Throws:
      IDNotRecognisedException - if incident ID is not recognised
      IllegalStateException - if incident cannot be cancelled due to its current state

    • escalateIncident

      public void escalateIncident(int incidentId, int newSeverity) throws IDNotRecognisedException, InvalidSeverityException, IllegalStateException
      Escalates the severity of the incident which must be between 1 and 5, and the incident must not be resolved or cancelled.
      Specified by:
      escalateIncident in interface CityRescue
      Parameters:
      incidentId - the unique identification of the incident to escalate
      newSeverity - new severity level ranging from 1 to 5
      Throws:
      IDNotRecognisedException - if incident ID is not recognised
      InvalidSeverityException - if severity level is not between 1 and 5
      IllegalStateException - if incident is resolved or cancelled

    • getIncidentIds

      public int[] getIncidentIds()
      Returns the IDs of the incidents in ascending order.
      Specified by:
      getIncidentIds in interface CityRescue
      Returns:
      an array of the incident ids

    • viewIncident

      public String viewIncident(int incidentId) throws IDNotRecognisedException
      Returns a string with the incident ID, the incident type, the severity, the location of the incident, the status of the incident and the unit ID to which the current incident is resolved by.
      Specified by:
      viewIncident in interface CityRescue
      Parameters:
      incidentId - the unique identification of the incident to view
      Returns:
      a string containing information regarding the incident
      Throws:
      IDNotRecognisedException - if the incident ID is not recognised

    • dispatch

      public void dispatch()
      Dispatches an available unit to the reported incident. Depends on which unit has the shortest Manhattan distance, to the location the incident is taking place in.
      Specified by:
      dispatch in interface CityRescue

    • tick

      public void tick()
      Introduces a tick system which keeps track of the time units spend at incident locations and solving them, updates the units status during, after an incident tracking their work progress in the meantime. If a unit resolves an incident, it is marked as resolved
      Specified by:
      tick in interface CityRescue

    • getStatus

      public String getStatus()
      Returns the status of the city which includes the number of stations, units, incidents, obstacles, and the ticks. It summarises that state of the rescue system.
      Specified by:
      getStatus in interface CityRescue
      Returns:
      a string which illustrates the status of the rescue system
